channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Southern California community among safest small town in America: report
item.description: MoneyGeek, a personal finance site, had researchers analyze last year’s FBI crime data of more than 1,000 small towns and cities where the population is between 30,000 and 100,000.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: After predicting the financial growth of 2024, here's what 2 economists see in 2025
item.description: Brian Blank is a finance scholar and Fed watcher who researches how companies navigate downturns and make financial decisions, as well as how markets process information.
item.dc:creator: KREX 5

item.title: MAGA civil war erupts between Musk, critics over H-1B visas
item.description: A Trump World civil war has erupted over visas for highly skilled workers, with the president-elect’s new tech industry allies like Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy on one side and the anti-immigration MAGA base on the other.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Abortions are up in the US. It's a complicated picture as women turn to pills, travel
item.description: Even with abortion bans in place in most Republican-controlled states, the number of people obtaining them has grown slightly.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kevin S. Vineys, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Explosion at Tyson Foods plant leaves 1 dead, others injured
item.description: An explosion at a Tyson Foods plant in Georgia late Thursday night left one person dead and others injured.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walmart delivery driver caught pleasuring himself in Horry County woman's driveway after dropping off groceries, lawsuit claims
item.description: HORRY COUNTY, S.C. (WBTW) -- A woman has filed a lawsuit in Horry County, South Carolina alleging that she caught a Walmart delivery driver pleasuring himself inside his vehicle in her driveway after dropping off her groceries.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina McDonald's employee fatally shoots DoorDash driver, police say
item.description: A McDonald's employee who was on the loose after a shooting that killed a DoorDash delivery driver Thursday night has been taken into custody.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Sockol, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge signals that contempt hearing for Rudy Giuliani over his assets might not go well for him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge is signaling that Rudy Giuliani’s contempt hearing next Friday might not end so well for the former New York City mayor and onetime personal lawyer for President-elect Donald Trump as two Georgia election poll workers try to collect a $148 million defamation award the
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Body of Arizona good Samaritan found near smoldering vehicle on dirt road
item.description: A man dubbed a good Samaritan by authorities was found dead next to a burning vehicle in Arizona on Christmas Eve, investigators have said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: Pennsylvania teacher jailed in Russia formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US homelessness up 18% as affordable housing remains out of reach for many people
item.description: The United States saw an 18.1% increase in homelessness this year, a dramatic rise driven mostly by a lack of affordable housing as well as devastating natural disasters and a surge of migrants in several parts of the country, federal officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on a Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4

item.title: Mexico tests app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: U.S. authorities are obliged to give notice to home-country consulates when a foreign citizen is detained. Mexico says it has beefed up consular staff and legal aid to help migrants in the legal process related to deportation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles building from famous rock album cover goes up in flames
item.description: Once featured on the cover of a Doors album, the former site of the Morrison Hotel was destroyed by a fire on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, ABC 4

item.title: Onion-wielding man disrupts Christmas church services, pours whiskey into holy water: deputies
item.description: A Maryland man was arrested and charged with several offences stemming from disruptions at two Catholic churches on Christmas Eve, including one disruption which he is accused of pouring liquor into a container of holy water.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: US to send $1.25 billion in weapons to Ukraine, pushing to get aid out before Biden leaves office
item.description: U.S. officials say the United States is expected to announce it will send another $1.25 billion in military assistance to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: VIDEO: Dog, man stranded in rough waters get rescued on Christmas
item.description: A Christmas morning boat trip for a man and his dog turned into a rescue mission after they got stranded in the Pacific Ocean's rough seas.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Massachusetts family found dead from carbon monoxide exposure
item.description: A family from Newton, Massachusetts, have been identified as the victims found dead inside a home on Christmas Day after an apparent exposure to carbon monoxide.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kay Granger revelation punctuates increasing scrutiny of aging officials
item.description: The revelation that Rep. Kay Granger (R-Texas) has been living in an assisted living facility is fueling scrutiny of aging public officials, highlighting the shifting norms surrounding lawmaker health, seniority, and for how long it is appropriate to hang on to power.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Most blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death: poll
item.description: Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: LA building from famous rock album cover goes up in flames
item.description: Once featured on the cover of a Doors album, the former site of the Morrison Hotel was destroyed by a fire on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: Climate-friendly electricity sees big battery projects soar again for 2024
item.description: 2024 was another banner year for a source of electricity that is better for people's lungs, better for climate change and may be reaching your home when you turn on the lights or turn up the thermostat — large banks of batteries.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nuclear bunker sales up in 2024, but experts warn not a solution
item.description: Sales for private underground bunkers, or fallout shelters, increased this year, and they're predicted to grow.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Getting too close to first responders will soon be a crime in Florida
item.description: Florida will soon have a so-called “Halo Law” meant to protect first responders. The new law, starting in January, creates a 25 feet buffer zone around law enforcement officers, firefighters, and emergency medical crews.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump has pressed for voting changes. GOP majorities in Congress will try to make that happen
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Republicans plan to move quickly in their effort to overhaul the nation’s voting procedures, seeing an opportunity with control of the White House and both chambers of Congress to push through long-sought changes that include voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump announces ambassador to Panama amid fight over canal
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced Miami-Dade County Commissioner Kevin Marino Cabrera as his pick for U.S. ambassador to Panama. The announcement comes as Trump is sparking a feud with the U.S. ally over control of the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trial of man accused in Trump assassination attempt in Florida pushed back to September
item.description: A man accused of attempting to assassinate President-elect Donald Trump in South Florida won't be tried until September 2025, a federal judge ruled this week.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stunning photos show lava erupting from Hawaii's Kilauea volcano
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the world's most active volcanoes spewed lava into the air for a second straight day on Tuesday. The eruption of Kilauea volcano on Hawaii's Big Island has stayed within the mountain's summit caldera inside Hawaii Volcanoes National Park. No homes were threatened.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione pleads not guilty to state charges in death of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: The man accused of fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare pleaded not guilty on Monday to murder and terror charges in a state case that will run parallel to his federal prosecution.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lara Trump says she will not seek to replace Rubio in Senate
item.description: The incoming president announced Rubio as his Secretary of State choice in November. Earlier this month, Lara Trump stepped down as RNC co-chair to “heavily consider” taking on the role in Congress's upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9
